margin_semantics: measurement-noise margin (minimum resolvable difference): the 95th-percentile null difference between two equally good models given these two series' draw-to-draw noise; derived from each series' own draws (split-half nulls), pair-specific.
delta_convention: delta = selected - baseline, computed per expression, then averaged
leaderboard_semantics: marginal value + 95% CI of each series AT EXACTLY t seconds per problem (median): per-problem linear interpolation in log10-time between bracketing measured configurations; a ladder ending below t carries its last measured value forward (status 'plateau', a lower bound under monotone quality-in-compute — never a trend extrapolation). NEVER difference two rows — head-to-head questions belong to the paired records.

notes: v23-val is drawn from flash-ansr's training distribution: in-distribution for flash-ansr-family series, foreign for baselines.
